title: Storage optimisation now survives rapid reloads
summary: The one-time database conversion that speeds up cold opens is atomic, so reloading mid-run used to cancel it with zero progress — and the slowness it fixes is exactly what makes people reload. xNet now remembers interrupted attempts: it shows a subtle "keep this tab open" hint while the optimisation is running again, and after repeated interruptions it starts the work right at first paint so the conversion finally lands.
highlights: Interrupted storage-optimisation attempts are tracked and retried smarter
Subtle non-blocking hint asks you to keep the tab open while it finishes
After repeated interruptions the optimisation starts right after first paint
